workflow.name: Generate and schedule social media posts with OpenAI, Google Sheets and Buffer
workflow.description:
Automate your social media content pipeline from idea to scheduled post. This workflow reads content ideas from a Google Sheet, uses OpenAI to generate platform-optimized posts for LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Facebook, then schedules them via Buffer — all with Slack notifications and error tracking.

Key features:
- Multi-platform optimization: Tailors content length, tone, and hashtags for each social network.
- Automated scheduling: Direct integration with Buffer for hands-off publishing.
- Robust error handling: Updates your sheet with failure logs and alerts Slack if something goes wrong.
- Batch processing: Handles multiple content rows in a single execution.

What's next:
- Add human-in-the-loop verification before publishing.
- Add multiple data sources (e.g., Reddit, Medium).

workflow.name: Generate and schedule social media posts with OpenAI, Google Sheets, and Buffer

values.content:
=You are an expert LinkedIn content creator. Create engaging, professional posts that drive engagement.

Rules:
- Start with a hook (question, bold statement, or statistic)
- Use short paragraphs (2-3 sentences max)
- Include 3-5 relevant hashtags at the end
- Add a clear call-to-action
- Keep total length under 1300 characters
- Use emojis sparingly (1-3 max)
- Write in {{ $('Process Each Item').item.json.tone ?? 'professional' }} tone

Output ONLY the post content, nothing else.

=You are an expert Twitter/X content creator. Create viral, engaging tweets that maximize engagement.

Rules:
- Maximum 280 characters (STRICT LIMIT)
- Start with a hook
- Use 1-3 relevant hashtags
- Be concise and punchy
- Write in {{ $('Process Each Item').item.json.tone ?? 'professional' }} tone
- Include a call-to-action when possible

Output ONLY the tweet, nothing else. MUST be under 280 characters.

=You are an expert Facebook content creator. Create engaging posts that encourage comments and shares.

Rules:
- Conversational and friendly tone
- Ask a question to encourage engagement
- Use 1-2 emojis naturally
- Keep under 500 characters for optimal engagement
- Write in {{ $('Process Each Item').item.json.tone ?? 'casual' }} tone
- Include a soft call-to-action

Output ONLY the post content, nothing else.

parameters.name: scheduled_at
parameters.value: ={{ $('Collect All Platform Posts').first().json.schedule_date }}T{{ $('Collect All Platform Posts').first().json.schedule_time }}:00Z

parameters.name: scheduled_at
parameters.value: ={{ $('Collect All Platform Posts').first().json.schedule_date }}T{{ $('Collect All Platform Posts').first().json.schedule_time }}:00Z

parameters.content:
## How it works
This workflow runs daily at 6 AM UTC. It reads all rows with status "pending" from your Google Sheet, validates that required fields are present (content ID, topic, key points, schedule date, and time), then processes each row one at a time.

For each row, OpenAI generates three platform-specific posts: a professional LinkedIn post (under 1,300 characters with hashtags), a concise Twitter/X post (under 280 characters), and a conversational Facebook post (under 500 characters). The generated content is written back to the sheet, then scheduled to each platform via the Buffer API.

After all items are processed, a Slack summary is sent. If any step fails, the error is logged to the sheet and a Slack alert is triggered.

## Setup steps
1. Create four n8n variables in Settings > Variables: `GOOGLE_SHEET_URL`, `BUFFER_LINKEDIN_PROFILE_ID`, `BUFFER_TWITTER_PROFILE_ID`, `BUFFER_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_ID`.
2. Connect your **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential to the sheet nodes.
3. Add your **OpenAI API** credential to all three generation nodes.
4. Create an **HTTP Header Auth** credential for Buffer with your access token.
5. Connect your **Slack OAuth2** credential and set the notification channel.
6. Ensure your Google Sheet has these columns: `content_id`, `topic`, `key_points`, `tone`, `target_audience`, `platforms`, `schedule_date`, `schedule_time`, `status`, `linkedin_post`, `twitter_post`, `facebook_post`, `error_log`.

**Trigger & fetch**
Daily schedule + Google Sheets read

**AI content generation**
Sequential: LinkedIn > Twitter > Facebook

**Scheduling & status**
Buffer API calls + Google Sheets updates

**Error handling**
Log failures to sheet + Slack alert

data.details: In n8n, when a workflow execution fails, it can start another workflow. This second workflow can be any arbitrary workflow on your n8n instance. Use the Error Trigger node as your Trigger in the Error workflow.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
